title: Never Inject Like This! The best & worst nasolabial fold injection points.
filename: Never Inject Like This! The best & worst nasolabial fold injection points..en.vtt
excerpt: Why don't I inject like this recently one of my traines sent me this video and asked me this question I'll be honest the mental model that I have about how the anatomy in the face Works makes me cringe When I See This injection before we dive in I've created something new that I …

segments.text: why don't I inject like this recently one of my traines sent me this video and asked me this question I'll be honest the mental model that I have about how the anatomy in the face Works makes me cringe When I See This injection before we dive in I've created something new that I think many of you going to love if you want to learn systematically cover all the injections processes and all the information that you need to be a topnotch injector ofme toxins make sure you click on the link that comes with this video to find out more so what I would like to do in this video is share in a great deal of detail why I would never inject this way to help you build up your own mental model of how the anatomy works and how small things really matter depth position angle can completely change the likelihood of causing a vascular clusion and the aesthetic result that you get so let's have a look in detail at this injection point the thing that's unusual for me is the way that the entry point is above the actual nasal abial fold so we are now injecting just lateral to the A crease and we are projecting the middle

part of the cheek outwards slightly above the nasal abial fold now there are multiple things here that with the little context that we've got don't quite make sense to me firstly which is why would you want the projection in this particular area usually we're trying to increase the volume in the nasal abial fold and decrease the relative size of the cheek in this area whereas in this case we're actually projecting that part of the cheek outwards so the aesthetic result is something that I'm not particularly keenon that's much more subjective though than the anatomy remembering our main purpose when injecting is to try and get the result without causing an injury so you can see that there is no aspiration going on in this video and people do argue about the sensitivity of aspiration but there's also a bolus technique going on so if you do a high volume injection with minimal safety checks of course I don't know whether ultrasound was used first so that might have changed things but a high volume injection without aspirating is probably more risky than a high volume volum injection with aspirating not to say that aspirating means you never get a

vascular clusion but it does detect about 50% of the time when you're intravascular so let's now look at the anatomy so that you can understand why I would not inject this way if you look at the vessels on the face we have in this area quite a few blood vessels within about a centimeter and a half of the injection point the most important of which is probably not the one that you see first because most people would think about the facial artery now the facial artery in this area tends to run in the fat so it's usually not on the periosteum which means so long as you're deep you're going to be safe and that's probably the main thing that this injector is using in terms of safety is to be at the right depth now the problem is because in my view because they are so Superior they're actually outside of the nasal abial fold look how much closer that brings them to the infraorbital artery now the infraorbital artery passes its vessels from lateral more to medial and if you ever do a kadava course these are the vessels that you find usually on the bone because they have literally just emerged from the infraorbital framan on the bone you

will often still find vessels at this depth and this is my main critique of this injection point which is there is very clearly a risk of getting some of those infraorbital vessels now when treating the nasal Abal fold this is one of the side effects which I have seen a severe vascular cusion affecting the infraorbital artery which ultimately affects the blood flow in the maxillary artery which supplied in this in the case that I remember the soft pallet and the NASA fank so it's a nasty internal vascular cusion when a high volume injection is injected into the infraorbital artery and that's the risk that I think this style of injecting is most increasing so this is how I'm imagining that this particular injection working if I move the needle to the side you'll see where the needle is touching the periostium and we are in theory underneath the facial artery but we're very close to the branches of the infraorbital artery because we are so much higher up than my normal injection point which would be much more inferior lower down over here we have Superior label artery which will be a

long way from this injection the facial artery which will be above it and the infraorbital artery here which is the one most at risk we also have the lateral nasal artery where the entry point is you're a little bit more likely I believe to skew these arteries on the way in so you might likely go through the facial artery or a branch of the lateral nasal artery and this is the other part of this injection which is in my opinion too Superior that is likely to cause problems if you did it on everyone all the time so now that I've shared how I think injection Point Precision changes your overall risk and how this more Superior entry point increases the chance of affecting the infraorbital artery and potentially just causing increased bruising through skewing either the facial artery or the lateral nasal artery you understand a bit more about the details of anatomy that go through my mind when I'm deciding how to inject and this should help you make more nuanced decisions when you're injecting about your depth position angle and volume and whether you aspirate for example overall

hopefully this will help everyone become safer and at least more confident when you're injecting that you're thinking about all the variables and not just one aesthetic result or just one artery but the overall slightly more complex Anatomy which we all should know if you're interested in learning Anatomy on a much more detailed way in a much more fun way then make sure you click the link in the description of this video to find out more [Music]
